plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-568 RP18.P05.M06.S19-RP18.P06.M02.S20 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-568 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP18 p05 closeout p06 foundation: the P05 Synthetic Fixture Set tail (the four remaining rows), the full P05 Test And Golden Case Set, Hermes Evidence Packet, Claude Review Packet, and Closeout And Next Handoff cycles, the full P06 Scope Inventory and Contract Draft cycles, and the P06 Type And Shape Definition head (the first twenty rows) as eight descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H18), Claude review packet (C18), and CP00-569 handoff, without opening AI legal workflows, precedent, clause markup, or DD extraction runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-568 is a descriptor-only AI Legal Workflows (RP18) closeout pack covering 150 planned units RP18.P05.M06.S19-RP18.P06.M02.S20 across eight descriptor-only sections. Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is honestly scoped and fully supported: command-evidence.json and manifest.json both report production_ready:false with status implemented_pending_review, so there is no production/enterprise-trust overclaim. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced in code and contract: freezeCp568Result (service.js:2173) and CP568_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (registry.js:1599) set every runtime/dispatch/write flag to false with synthetic_only and no_real_data true, and the contract's current_pack, no_write_attestation, cp568_requirements, and cp568_no_write_attestation deep-equal the registry binding (validate script asserts and passed). Claude remains a read-only reviewer and not final approver: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, read_only=true, tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob (enforced and asserted in model.test.js:1215/1232). Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic review packet/receipt descriptors (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; production_ready_candidate is gated only on coverage+slice validity, not runtime). Coverage validation (validators.js:3181) plus model.test.js and the contract validator cross-check the 150-unit count and the deliverable/micro-title/phase distributions against the live plan, matching the prompt baseline exactly. Review-hardening preconditions hold: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1248-byte valid-JSON stdout), forbidden_review_evidence and hardened_review_sequence are defined, no invalid attempts are recorded for this pack, and the two unrelated dirty UI files (progress-control-room) are correctly excluded from active pack scope. All listed validation commands report exit_code 0. No P0/P1/P2/P3 issues found.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-568 ai legal workflows core p05 closeout p06 foundation descriptor pack
